description:
Router._sendEth(address,uint256) (src/Router.sol#237-240) sends eth to arbitrary user
	Dangerous calls:
	- (ok,None) = to.call{value: amount}() (src/Router.sol#238)
disposition: FALSE-POSITIVE. Router._sendEth is `private` and reached from exactly one site (createToken, the creation-fee push) with `factoryContract.treasury()` — a factory-configured address, NEVER caller-supplied (spec §4.1). Guarded by nonReentrant + CEI. Not an arbitrary send. contracts.md §2.4/§5.6.

description:
Reentrancy in V3Migrator._mintAndSettle(V3Migrator.Ctx) (src/V3Migrator.sol#342-384):
	External call allowing reentrancy:
	- _mint(m) (src/V3Migrator.sol#375)
		- (m.tokenId,m.liquidity,m.used0,m.used1) = INonfungiblePositionManager(positionManager).mint(INonfungiblePositionManager.MintParams({token0:m.token0,token1:m.token1,fee:FEE_TIER,tickLower:TICK_LOWER,tickUpper:TICK_UPPER,amount0Desired:m.amt0Desired,amount1Desired:m.amt1Desired,amount0Min:m.amt0Min,amount1Min:m.amt1Min,recipient:vault,deadline:block.timestamp})) (src/V3Migrator.sol#389-404)
	Balance read before the call:
	- wethBal = IERC20(weth_).balanceOf(address(this)) (src/V3Migrator.sol#354)
	Possible stale balance used after the call in a condition:
	- m.liquidity == 0 (src/V3Migrator.sol#376)
		- stale variable `REF_439`
disposition: FALSE-POSITIVE. The flagged external call is NPM.mint() to the real, trusted, immutable Uniswap NonfungiblePositionManager (§12.28 pinned address, asserted at deploy). The 'stale' tokenBal/wethBal feed only amount0/1Desired and the post-mint `m.liquidity==0` guard (InsufficientLiquidityMinted — defense-in-depth). LaunchToken & WETH have no transfer hooks; the only reentrancy surface (the pool swap callback) is closed by the `_activePool` guard in _arbToTarget, which has fully returned before mint. No attacker-controlled reentry. contracts.md §2.5/§5.4.

description:
Reentrancy in V3Migrator._mintAndSettle(V3Migrator.Ctx) (src/V3Migrator.sol#342-384):
	External call allowing reentrancy:
	- _mint(m) (src/V3Migrator.sol#375)
		- (m.tokenId,m.liquidity,m.used0,m.used1) = INonfungiblePositionManager(positionManager).mint(INonfungiblePositionManager.MintParams({token0:m.token0,token1:m.token1,fee:FEE_TIER,tickLower:TICK_LOWER,tickUpper:TICK_UPPER,amount0Desired:m.amt0Desired,amount1Desired:m.amt1Desired,amount0Min:m.amt0Min,amount1Min:m.amt1Min,recipient:vault,deadline:block.timestamp})) (src/V3Migrator.sol#389-404)
	Balance read before the call:
	- tokenBal = IERC20(c.token).balanceOf(address(this)) (src/V3Migrator.sol#353)
	Possible stale balance used after the call in a condition:
	- m.liquidity == 0 (src/V3Migrator.sol#376)
		- stale variable `REF_439`
disposition: FALSE-POSITIVE. The flagged external call is NPM.mint() to the real, trusted, immutable Uniswap NonfungiblePositionManager (§12.28 pinned address, asserted at deploy). The 'stale' tokenBal/wethBal feed only amount0/1Desired and the post-mint `m.liquidity==0` guard (InsufficientLiquidityMinted — defense-in-depth). LaunchToken & WETH have no transfer hooks; the only reentrancy surface (the pool swap callback) is closed by the `_activePool` guard in _arbToTarget, which has fully returned before mint. No attacker-controlled reentry. contracts.md §2.5/§5.4.

description:
V3Migrator._mintAndSettle(V3Migrator.Ctx) (src/V3Migrator.sol#342-384) uses a dangerous strict equality:
	- m.liquidity == 0 (src/V3Migrator.sol#376)
disposition: FALSE-POSITIVE. `m.liquidity==0` (revert InsufficientLiquidityMinted) and `budget==0` (revert ArbBudgetExceeded) are exact zero-guards on integers derived from trusted V3/NPM returns and in-contract math — strict equality is the correct and intended semantics, not a dangerous balance/timestamp equality.

description:
V3Migrator._arbStep(V3Migrator.Ctx,uint256,uint256) (src/V3Migrator.sol#283-320) uses a dangerous strict equality:
	- budget == 0 (src/V3Migrator.sol#311)
disposition: FALSE-POSITIVE. `m.liquidity==0` (revert InsufficientLiquidityMinted) and `budget==0` (revert ArbBudgetExceeded) are exact zero-guards on integers derived from trusted V3/NPM returns and in-contract math — strict equality is the correct and intended semantics, not a dangerous balance/timestamp equality.

description:
Reentrancy in CurveFactory.createToken(address,string,string,bytes32,string) (src/CurveFactory.sol#232-264):
	External calls:
	- pool = IV3Migrator(migrator).initializePool(token) (src/CurveFactory.sol#252)
	State variables written after the call(s):
	- ++ tokenCounter (src/CurveFactory.sol#259)
	CurveFactory.tokenCounter (src/CurveFactory.sol#134) can be used in cross function reentrancies:
	- CurveFactory.createToken(address,string,string,bytes32,string) (src/CurveFactory.sol#232-264)
	- CurveFactory.tokenCounter (src/CurveFactory.sol#134)
disposition: FALSE-POSITIVE. The pre-write external call is to the trusted, immutable factory (CurveFactory.createToken→migrator.initializePool; BondingCurve.buy→factory.recordEthDelta). initializePool only touches the V3 factory/pool and recordEthDelta is a bounded accumulator — neither calls back into the flagged contract. Both entrypoints are nonReentrant (createToken is onlyRouter behind the nonReentrant Router; buy is nonReentrant) so cross-function reentry is impossible. State written after the call is pre-computed before it. (BondingCurve.buy: recordEthDelta is an interaction slightly ahead of the effects block — acknowledged CEI-ordering nuance, safe because the callee is trusted and cannot reenter.) contracts.md §5.4.

description:
Reentrancy in BondingCurve.buy(address,address,address,uint256) (src/BondingCurve.sol#154-221):
	External calls:
	- ICurveFactory(factory).recordEthDelta(int256(net)) (src/BondingCurve.sol#194)
	State variables written after the call(s):
	- _realEthReserves = newRealEth (src/BondingCurve.sol#207)
	BondingCurve._realEthReserves (src/BondingCurve.sol#108) can be used in cross function reentrancies:
	- BondingCurve.quoteBuy(uint256) (src/BondingCurve.sol#308-330)
	- BondingCurve.reserves() (src/BondingCurve.sol#341-348)
	- phase = Phase.ReadyToGraduate (src/BondingCurve.sol#211)
	BondingCurve.phase (src/BondingCurve.sol#114) can be used in cross function reentrancies:
	- BondingCurve.constructor() (src/BondingCurve.sol#121-149)
	- BondingCurve.phase (src/BondingCurve.sol#114)
	- BondingCurve.quoteBuy(uint256) (src/BondingCurve.sol#308-330)
	- BondingCurve.quoteSell(uint256) (src/BondingCurve.sol#333-338)
disposition: FALSE-POSITIVE. The pre-write external call is to the trusted, immutable factory (CurveFactory.createToken→migrator.initializePool; BondingCurve.buy→factory.recordEthDelta). initializePool only touches the V3 factory/pool and recordEthDelta is a bounded accumulator — neither calls back into the flagged contract. Both entrypoints are nonReentrant (createToken is onlyRouter behind the nonReentrant Router; buy is nonReentrant) so cross-function reentry is impossible. State written after the call is pre-computed before it. (BondingCurve.buy: recordEthDelta is an interaction slightly ahead of the effects block — acknowledged CEI-ordering nuance, safe because the callee is trusted and cannot reenter.) contracts.md §5.4.

description: V3Migrator.migrate(address).c (src/V3Migrator.sol#231) is a local variable never initialized
disposition: FALSE-POSITIVE. `Ctx memory c` and `MintLocals memory m` are structs whose fields are all populated before first read; `wethArbSpent` and `refund` are uint256 intentionally starting at 0 (arb accumulator / no-refund default). Standard Slither memory-struct false positive.

description: Router._deploy(string,string,bytes32,string) (src/Router.sol#124-129) ignores return value by (token,curve,None) = factoryContract.createToken(msg.sender,name,symbol,metadataHash,metadataUri) (src/Router.sol#128)
disposition: REVIEWED — INTENTIONAL. Router create/buy/sell/quote + _deploy discard tuple fields that are either re-emitted elsewhere (pool addr in factory TokenCreated; acceptedEthGross/fee in curve Trade) or not needed by the deliberately-thin Router; no security decision depends on a dropped value. V3Migrator slot0() partial-destructures to read only sqrtPriceX96/tick (rest of slot0 genuinely unused). V3Migrator pool.swap() return is unneeded — spend is measured via WETH balance delta and the swap is price-capped to targetSqrt. BondingCurve.graduate discards migrate()'s (tokenId,liquidity); the migrator emits Graduated. contracts.md §2.4/§2.5.

description:
V3Migrator._arbStep(V3Migrator.Ctx,uint256,uint256) (src/V3Migrator.sol#283-320) has external calls inside a loop: IUniswapV3Pool(c.pool).swap(address(this),zeroForOne,_toInt256(budget),c.targetSqrt,) (src/V3Migrator.sol#314)
	Calls stack containing the loop:
		V3Migrator.migrate(address)
		V3Migrator._arbToTarget(V3Migrator.Ctx)
disposition: INTENTIONAL / ACKNOWLEDGED. The bounded arb-back loop (MAX_ARB_ITERATIONS) inherently queries slot0()/balances and calls swap() per iteration — iterative price correction of a pre-seeded pool (spec §6.3.2). Bounded iteration count → no unbounded-gas griefing; every call targets the trusted V3 pool / WETH. Budget exhaustion reverts cleanly (curve stays ReadyToGraduate, retriable) — never a hostile mint.

description:
Reentrancy in CurveFactory.createToken(address,string,string,bytes32,string) (src/CurveFactory.sol#232-264):
	External calls:
	- pool = IV3Migrator(migrator).initializePool(token) (src/CurveFactory.sol#252)
	State variables written after the call(s):
	- curveOf[token] = curve (src/CurveFactory.sol#255)
	- isCurve[curve] = true (src/CurveFactory.sol#257)
	- tokenOf[curve] = token (src/CurveFactory.sol#256)
disposition: BENIGN (Slither's own classification). State written after a call to a trusted contract (factory/migrator/NPM) with no security relevance and no attacker reentry path (nonReentrant entrypoints; trusted callees; no token hooks). Same trust analysis as reentrancy-no-eth above.

description:

description:
Reentrancy in V3Migrator.migrate(address) (src/V3Migrator.sol#228-258):
	External calls:
	- IWETH9(weth).deposit{value: msg.value}() (src/V3Migrator.sol#242)
	- _arbToTarget(c) (src/V3Migrator.sol#250)
		- IUniswapV3Pool(c.pool).swap(address(this),zeroForOne,_toInt256(budget),c.targetSqrt,) (src/V3Migrator.sol#314)
	- (tokenId,liquidity) = _mintAndSettle(c) (src/V3Migrator.sol#257)
		- (m.tokenId,m.liquidity,m.used0,m.used1) = INonfungiblePositionManager(positionManager).mint(INonfungiblePositionManager.MintParams({token0:m.token0,token1:m.token1,fee:FEE_TIER,tickLower:TICK_LOWER,tickUpper:TICK_UPPER,amount0Desired:m.amt0Desired,amount1Desired:m.amt1Desired,amount0Min:m.amt0Min,amount1Min:m.amt1Min,recipient:vault,deadline:block.timestamp})) (src/V3Migrator.sol#389-404)
	External calls sending eth:
	- IWETH9(weth).deposit{value: msg.value}() (src/V3Migrator.sol#242)
	Event emitted after the call(s):
	- Graduated(c.token,c.pool,m.tokenId,m.liquidity,m.wethInPosition,m.tokensInPosition,c.gradFee,tx.origin,IBondingCurve(msg.sender).CALLER_REWARD(),m.tokensBurned,m.wethDust) (src/V3Migrator.sol#430-442)
		- (tokenId,liquidity) = _mintAndSettle(c) (src/V3Migrator.sol#257)
disposition: FALSE-POSITIVE. Events (Trade/Graduated/PoolInitialized/FeesCollected) are emitted after an external call to a trusted contract; emission ordering affects only log ordering, never funds or control flow. LPFeeVault.collect emits after NPM.collect (NPM trusted, §12.28).

description:
BondingCurve.buy(address,address,address,uint256) (src/BondingCurve.sol#154-221) uses timestamp for comparisons
	Dangerous comparisons:
	- block.timestamp < EARLY_WINDOW_END && grossIn > MAX_EARLY_BUY (src/BondingCurve.sol#167)
disposition: REQUIRED BY SPEC — NOT A VULN. BondingCurve.buy compares `block.timestamp < EARLY_WINDOW_END` for the anti-sniper window. Using block.timestamp is MANDATED: block.number is banned on Orbit (spec §2/§6.5, CLAUDE.md hard rule). ~100ms blocks vs a seconds-scale window make sub-second sequencer skew immaterial; the acknowledged bypass is multi-wallet, not timestamp gaming (contracts.md §5.2).
